visa_types.name: Residence Card For Family Member Of EU/EEA National
visa_types.documents_required: For checklists in family immigration cases, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/).
visa_types.visa_fees:
For Visa fee list click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
The Service fee of 26 Euros (to be paid in NIS) in cash at the Visa Application Centre and is non-refundable.
Residence Card for Family Member of EU/EEA national has no application fee. You have to fill in the form Application for a permit for residence or work and please contact the Norwegian embassy in Amman by email first (visa.amman@mfa.no (mailto:visa.amman@mfa.no)), in order to get information about how to proceed.
The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 23.4 Euro (Per application) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres.
Courier fee is 17.5 Euro Per application
visa_types.processing_time: The waiting time at (UDI) the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration Click Here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)

click here
.

Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)
.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are from a country outside the EU/EEA and you want to join a family member in Norway who is an EU/EEA national, you can apply for a residence card for family members of EU/EEA nationals.
Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply for more information. For information about who is eligible to apply for a visa at the Norway Visa Application Centre, please see Jurisdiction to Apply.

																	
You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman. (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are from a country outside the EU/EEA and you want to join a family member in Norway who is an EU/EEA national, you can apply for a residence card for family members of EU/EEA nationals.
Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply for more information. For information about who is eligible to apply for a visa at the Norway Visa Application Centre, please see Jurisdiction to Apply.

																	
You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman. (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/)

## Visa Fees
For Visa fee list click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
The Service fee of 26 Euros (to be paid in NIS) in cash at the Visa Application Centre and is non-refundable.
Residence Card for Family Member of EU/EEA national has no application fee. You have to fill in the form Application for a permit for residence or work and please contact the Norwegian embassy in Amman by email first (visa.amman@mfa.no (mailto:visa.amman@mfa.no)), in order to get information about how to proceed.
The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 23.4 Euro (Per application) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres.
Courier fee is 17.5 Euro Per application

## Documents Required
For checklists in family immigration cases, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/).

## Photo Specifications
Please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/) for the requirements for the photo that you must hand in with your application.

## Processing Time
The waiting time at (UDI) the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration Click Here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)

## Download Forms

- For the application form for a residence permit in Norway
click here
.
- For checklists in family immigration cases, please visit the website of the
Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)
.
- Form for Granting Power of Attorney (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/power-of-attorney--authorisation-/)

visa_types.name: Family Immigration
visa_types.documents_required:
- For the Document checklist please
click here (https://protect-eu.mimecast.com/s/tPAnC49Z9f8ymqWfxE8Us?domain=udi.no/)
For more information regarding personal documents, please check this list: https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf (https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf)
visa_types.visa_fees:
- You are advised to check the below link for more information:
 
Fees - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
For a complete list of fees, please see the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/). The fees are paid at the Application Portal Norway (https://selfservice.udi.no/). 

Please note: 
- VFS will charge a service fee of 26 Euro (To be paid in NIS) per application. This comes in addition to the visa fee and is non-refundable.
- The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres. 
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 23.4 Euro (Per application) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit/debit cards through the 
Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/system/logg-inn/)
.
The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres.
Courier fee is 17.5 Euro Per application

For more information regarding personal documents, please check this list: https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf (https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Family immigration is also called family reunification. Those who apply for family immigration are usually the spouse, cohabitant or child of someone who lives in Norway. Other types
                                                                        of family members who may apply are parents who have children in Norway, those who are going to marry someone in Norway (fiancées/fiancés), foster children and full siblings.  
Please see the websites of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration  (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) for more
                                                                        information about different family immigration permits. 
Click on each of the tabs above and on How to apply for more information about document requirements and application procedures.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Family immigration is also called family reunification. Those who apply for family immigration are usually the spouse, cohabitant or child of someone who lives in Norway. Other types
                                                                        of family members who may apply are parents who have children in Norway, those who are going to marry someone in Norway (fiancées/fiancés), foster children and full siblings.  
Please see the websites of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration  (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) for more
                                                                        information about different family immigration permits. 
Click on each of the tabs above and on How to apply for more information about document requirements and application procedures.  

## Visa Fees

- You are advised to check the below link for more information:
 
Fees - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
For a complete list of fees, please see the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/). The fees are paid at the Application Portal Norway (https://selfservice.udi.no/). 

Please note: 
- VFS will charge a service fee of 26 Euro (To be paid in NIS) per application. This comes in addition to the visa fee and is non-refundable.
- The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres. 
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 23.4 Euro (Per application) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit/debit cards through the 
Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/system/logg-inn/)
.
The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres.
Courier fee is 17.5 Euro Per application

## Documents Required
- For the Document checklist please
click here (https://protect-eu.mimecast.com/s/tPAnC49Z9f8ymqWfxE8Us?domain=udi.no/)
For more information regarding personal documents, please check this list: https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf (https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Everyone who applies for a visa or a residence permit must hand in a photo with their application. The photo must meet certain requirement, so that you are easily recognized. 
- The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
- Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
- Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
- the photo must be
- 
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note 
If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child. 
Illustration of photo requirements

## Processing Time
Please click to  see case processing times. (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/)

## Download Forms
- Checklists for required documentation for applications - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/ )
For more information regarding personal documents, please check this list: https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf (https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf)

visa_types.name: Study, Cultural and Sport Events
visa_types.documents_required:
- Political, Scientific, Cultural, Sports or Religious events Checklist -
CLICK HERE
- Training, Internship, Seminar, Course Checklist -
CLICK HERE
visa_types.visa_fees:
The fee for applying for a visitor’s visa (Short Term Visa – type C) is 90 Euros. Children between 6-12 years pay 45 Euros. However, you are advise to check the below link for more information: Fees - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
Please  note:
- You must pay the fee in the
Application Portal Norway (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/log-in/#undefined)
in order to register your application. However, you
                                                                            can inform the visa application centre when you submit your application if you think you are entitled to have the visa fee refunded by the Embassy.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit/debit cards through the Application Portal Norway.
- VFS will charge a service fee of 26 Euro (to be paid in NIS) per application.  This comes in addition to the visa fee and is non-refundable.
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 23.4 Euro (Per application) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres.
- Courier fee is 17.5 Euro Per application
visa_types.processing_time:
Please note that the processing time for short term visa application (C-Visa) is around 25 days - 15 days for handling the case, which starts from the day the Embassy in Amman receives the application, and 10 days for sending the application back and forth. Further, in some situations, for instance in case of border closure, or other unexpected logistical challenges, the courier time back and forth between Ramallah and Amman can be longer than 10 days. Hence, request you to kindly make your travel arrangements accordingly.
Most cases are processed by the embassy, but if the embassy is in doubt about whether you can be granted a visa, they will forward your case to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration in Oslo (UDI). In such cases, applicants will receive an answer within 45 days after the application is received and registered by the embassy.
Note that it will take a couple of days from when the case is submitted  at the application center, consulate or embassy until it is registered at the embassy. The waiting time starts from the day the application is registered at the embassy.
For more information, please check: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)
visa_types.forms:
The application form needs to be filled out online. Go to the Application Portal Norway (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/log-in/#undefined) to fill out the
                                                                        form and pay the application fee.
Checklist showing which documents you need to submit together with your application
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of travel is to attend courses, internships, participation in study/exchange programs/summer schools; participate in sports, training, cultural exchange programs, and participation in conferences, workshops, etc.

Please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx) for more information about your possibilities of being granted a visa.

A Visitor’s Visa (Schengen - type C) only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days.
Please click on each of the tabs above and on How to apply for more information about document requirements and application procedures. 

You can also find additional information about Visitor’s Visas on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The purpose of travel is to attend courses, internships, participation in study/exchange programs/summer schools; participate in sports, training, cultural exchange programs, and participation in conferences, workshops, etc.

Please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx) for more information about your possibilities of being granted a visa.

A Visitor’s Visa (Schengen - type C) only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days.
Please click on each of the tabs above and on How to apply for more information about document requirements and application procedures. 

You can also find additional information about Visitor’s Visas on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx). 

## Visa Fees
The fee for applying for a visitor’s visa (Short Term Visa – type C) is 90 Euros. Children between 6-12 years pay 45 Euros. However, you are advise to check the below link for more information: Fees - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
Please  note:
- You must pay the fee in the
Application Portal Norway (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/log-in/#undefined)
in order to register your application. However, you
                                                                            can inform the visa application centre when you submit your application if you think you are entitled to have the visa fee refunded by the Embassy.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit/debit cards through the Application Portal Norway.
- VFS will charge a service fee of 26 Euro (to be paid in NIS) per application.  This comes in addition to the visa fee and is non-refundable.
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 23.4 Euro (Per application) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres.
- Courier fee is 17.5 Euro Per application

## Documents Required
- Political, Scientific, Cultural, Sports or Religious events Checklist -
CLICK HERE
- Training, Internship, Seminar, Course Checklist -
CLICK HERE

## Photo Specifications
Everyone who applies for a visa or a residence permit must hand in a photo with their application. The photo must meet certain requirement, so that you are easily recognised. 
- The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
- Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
- Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
- the photo must be
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note 
If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child. 
Illustration of photo requirements

## Processing Time
Please note that the processing time for short term visa application (C-Visa) is around 25 days - 15 days for handling the case, which starts from the day the Embassy in Amman receives the application, and 10 days for sending the application back and forth. Further, in some situations, for instance in case of border closure, or other unexpected logistical challenges, the courier time back and forth between Ramallah and Amman can be longer than 10 days. Hence, request you to kindly make your travel arrangements accordingly.
Most cases are processed by the embassy, but if the embassy is in doubt about whether you can be granted a visa, they will forward your case to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration in Oslo (UDI). In such cases, applicants will receive an answer within 45 days after the application is received and registered by the embassy.
Note that it will take a couple of days from when the case is submitted  at the application center, consulate or embassy until it is registered at the embassy. The waiting time starts from the day the application is registered at the embassy.
For more information, please check: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)

## Download Forms
The application form needs to be filled out online. Go to the Application Portal Norway (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/log-in/#undefined) to fill out the
                                                                        form and pay the application fee.
Checklist showing which documents you need to submit together with your application

in order to register your application. However, you
                                                                            can inform the visa application centre when you submit your application if you think you are entitled to have the visa fee refunded by the Embassy.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit/debit cards through the Application Portal Norway.
- VFS will charge a service fee of 26 Euro (to be paid in NIS) per application. This comes in addition to the visa fee and is non-refundable.
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 23.4 Euro (Per application) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres.
- Courier fee is 17.5 Euro Per application
visa_types.processing_time:
Please note that the processing time for short term visa application (C-Visa) is around 25 days - 15 days for handling the case, which starts from the day the Embassy in Amman receives the application, and 10 days for sending the application back and forth. Further, in some situations, for instance in case of border closure, or other unexpected logistical challenges, the courier time back and forth between Ramallah and Amman can be longer than 10 days. Hence, request you to kindly make your travel arrangements accordingly.
Most cases are processed by the embassy, but if the embassy is in doubt about whether you can be granted a visa, they will forward your case to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration in Oslo (UDI). In such cases, applicants will receive an answer within 45 days after the application is received and registered by the embassy.
Note that it will take a couple of days from when the case is submitted  at the application center, consulate or embassy until it is registered at the embassy. The waiting time starts from the day the application is registered at the embassy.
For more information, please check: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)

                                                                        form and pay the application fee.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of the visit is to travel to Norway as a tourist, either by taking a trip organised by a tour operator, or through individual booking of tickets and accommodation. 
There is a very little chance of being granted a visa if you are a tourist. Please click here (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx) for more information about your possibilities of being granted a visa.
A Visitor’s Visa (Schengen - type C) only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days.
Please click on each of the tabs above and on How to apply for more information about document requirements and application procedures.   You can also find additional information
                                                                        about Visitor’s Visas on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI).  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The purpose of the visit is to travel to Norway as a tourist, either by taking a trip organised by a tour operator, or through individual booking of tickets and accommodation. 
There is a very little chance of being granted a visa if you are a tourist. Please click here (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx) for more information about your possibilities of being granted a visa.
A Visitor’s Visa (Schengen - type C) only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days.
Please click on each of the tabs above and on How to apply for more information about document requirements and application procedures.   You can also find additional information
                                                                        about Visitor’s Visas on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI).  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx)

## Visa Fees
The fee for applying for a visitor’s visa (Short Term Visa – type C) is 90 Euros. Children between 6-12 years pay 45 Euros. However, you are advise to check the below link for more information: Fees - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
Please  note:
- You must pay the fee in the
Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/system/logg-inn/)
in order to register your application. However, you
                                                                            can inform the visa application centre when you submit your application if you think you are entitled to have the visa fee refunded by the Embassy.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit/debit cards through the Application Portal Norway.
- VFS will charge a service fee of 26 Euro (to be paid in NIS) per application. This comes in addition to the visa fee and is non-refundable.
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 23.4 Euro (Per application) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres.
- Courier fee is 17.5 Euro Per application

## Documents Required
- Tourism Checklist -
CLICK HERE

## Photo Specifications
Everyone who applies for a visa or a residence permit must hand in a photo with their application. The photo must meet certain requirement, so that you are easily recognized. 
- The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
- Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
- Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
- the photo must be
- 
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note 
If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child. 
Illustration of photo requirements

## Processing Time
Please note that the processing time for short term visa application (C-Visa) is around 25 days - 15 days for handling the case, which starts from the day the Embassy in Amman receives the application, and 10 days for sending the application back and forth. Further, in some situations, for instance in case of border closure, or other unexpected logistical challenges, the courier time back and forth between Ramallah and Amman can be longer than 10 days. Hence, request you to kindly make your travel arrangements accordingly.
Most cases are processed by the embassy, but if the embassy is in doubt about whether you can be granted a visa, they will forward your case to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration in Oslo (UDI). In such cases, applicants will receive an answer within 45 days after the application is received and registered by the embassy.
Note that it will take a couple of days from when the case is submitted  at the application center, consulate or embassy until it is registered at the embassy. The waiting time starts from the day the application is registered at the embassy.
For more information, please check: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)

## Download Forms
The application form needs to be filled out online. Go to the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/system/logg-inn/) to fill out the
                                                                        form and pay the application fee.
                                                                    
Checklist showing which documents you need to submit together with your application

Further, in some situations, for instance in case of border closure, or other unexpected logistical challenges, the courier time back and forth between Ramallah and Amman can be longer than 10 days. Hence, request you to kindly make your travel arrangements accordingly.
Most cases are processed by the embassy, but if the embassy is in doubt about whether you can be granted a visa, they will forward your case to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration in Oslo (UDI). In such cases, applicants will receive an answer within 45 days after the application is received and registered by the embassy.
Note that it will take a couple of days from when the case is submitted  at the application center, consulate or embassy until it is registered at the embassy. The waiting time starts from the day the application is registered at the embassy.
For more information, please check: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of travel is usually visiting a close family member.
It is difficult to get a visa in order to visit family members. If you want to visit a more distant family member or a friend, it is very unlikely that you will be granted a visa. Please
                                                                        click here (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx) for more information about your possibilities
                                                                        of being granted a visa.
A Visitor’s Visa (Schengen - type C) only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days.
Please click on each of the tabs above and on How to apply for more information about document requirements and application procedures. The person you are going to visit will have
                                                                        to provide an invitation (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/invitation-for-a-visitors-visa-/) and in most cases a Sponsorship form (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/guarantee-for-applicants-visiting-on-a-visitors-visa-/).
You can also find additional information about Visitor’s Visas on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration  (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The purpose of travel is usually visiting a close family member.
It is difficult to get a visa in order to visit family members. If you want to visit a more distant family member or a friend, it is very unlikely that you will be granted a visa. Please
                                                                        click here (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx) for more information about your possibilities
                                                                        of being granted a visa.
A Visitor’s Visa (Schengen - type C) only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days.
Please click on each of the tabs above and on How to apply for more information about document requirements and application procedures. The person you are going to visit will have
                                                                        to provide an invitation (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/invitation-for-a-visitors-visa-/) and in most cases a Sponsorship form (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/guarantee-for-applicants-visiting-on-a-visitors-visa-/).
You can also find additional information about Visitor’s Visas on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration  (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/visit-and-holiday/visitors-visa-to-norway/?c=xxx)

## Visa Fees
The fee for applying for a visitor’s visa (Short Term Visa – type C) is 90 Euros. Children between 6-12 years pay 45 Euros. However, you are advise to check the below link for more information: Fees - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
Please  note:
- You must pay the fee in the
Application Portal Norway (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/log-in/#undefined)
in order to register your application. However, you

- VFS will charge a service fee of 26 Euro (to be paid in NIS) per application. This comes in addition to the visa fee and is non-refundable.
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 23.4 Euro (Per application) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres.
- Courier fee is 17.5 Euro Per application

## Documents Required

## Photo Specifications
Everyone who applies for a visa or a residence permit must hand in a photo with their application. The photo must meet certain requirement, so that you are easily recognised.
- The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
- Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
- Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
- the photo must be
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast

- taken directly from the front, against a light background

- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note 
If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child.

## Processing Time
Please note that the processing time for short term visa application (C-Visa) is around 25 days - 15 days for handling the case, which starts from the day the Embassy in Amman receives the application, and 10 days for sending the application back and forth. Further, in some situations, for instance in case of border closure, or other unexpected logistical challenges, the courier time back and forth between Ramallah and Amman can be longer than 10 days. Hence, request you to kindly make your travel arrangements accordingly.
Most cases are processed by the embassy, but if the embassy is in doubt about whether you can be granted a visa, they will forward your case to the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration in Oslo (UDI). In such cases, applicants will receive an answer within 45 days after the application is received and registered by the embassy.
Note that it will take a couple of days from when the case is submitted  at the application center, consulate or embassy until it is registered at the embassy. The waiting time starts from the day the application is registered at the embassy.
For more information, please check: https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/ (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)

Please note:

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to come to Norway to work, you need a residence permit. You must normally have found a job first. What residence permit you should apply for depends on your competence and
                                                                        the type of work you will be doing in Norway.
If you wish to study or go to school in Norway for more than three months, you must apply for a study permit. If you are granted a study permit, you will also be able to work for up to
                                                                        20 hours a week while you are studying and full-time during holidays.  
If you plan to travel to Norway to receive medical treatment over a period of more than 90 days, you must apply for a residence permit. If you need someone to accompany you, he or she can also be granted such a residence
                                                                        permit.
                                                                        
Please see the websites of the Norwegian  Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) for more information
                                                                        about different residence permits for work, study and other purposes. 
Click on each of the tabs above and on How to apply for more information about document requirements and application procedures.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to come to Norway to work, you need a residence permit. You must normally have found a job first. What residence permit you should apply for depends on your competence and
                                                                        the type of work you will be doing in Norway.
If you wish to study or go to school in Norway for more than three months, you must apply for a study permit. If you are granted a study permit, you will also be able to work for up to
                                                                        20 hours a week while you are studying and full-time during holidays.  
If you plan to travel to Norway to receive medical treatment over a period of more than 90 days, you must apply for a residence permit. If you need someone to accompany you, he or she can also be granted such a residence
                                                                        permit.
                                                                        
Please see the websites of the Norwegian  Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) for more information
                                                                        about different residence permits for work, study and other purposes. 
Click on each of the tabs above and on How to apply for more information about document requirements and application procedures. 

## Visa Fees
YOU CAN CHECK THE UDI WEBSITE
- You are advised to check the below link for more information:
Fees - UDI (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
For a complete list of fees, please see the website of the Norwegian Directorate of  Immigration (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/).
                                                                        The fees are paid at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/system/logg-inn/). 
Please note: 
- VFS will charge a service fee of 26 Euro (to be paid in NIS) per application. This comes in addition to the visa fee and is non-refundable.
- The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres.
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 23.4 Euro (Per application) to cover the Resubmission Courier.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit/debit cards through the
Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/system/logg-inn/)
.
The VFS service fee can only be paid in cash in NIS at the Visa Application Centres.
Courier fee is 17.5 Euro Per application

## Documents Required
- For the Document checklist please
click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)

## Photo Specifications
Everyone who applies for a visa or a residence permit must hand in a photo with their application. The photo must meet certain requirement, so that you are easily recognized. 
- The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
- Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
- Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
- the photo must be
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note 
If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child. 
Illustration of photo requirements

## Processing Time
Please click to see case processing times. (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/)

## Download Forms
Please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/system/logg-inn/) to register your application on the Application Portal Norway. 
Checklist showing which documents you need to submit together with your application (https://protect-eu.mimecast.com/s/tPAnC49Z9f8ymqWfxE8Us?domain=udi.no/)
